questions.question: After duplicating a public page, where is the duplicated page located in your sidebar?

options.text: The Public section
options.explanation: The Public section is for pages you have published to the web. Duplicated pages are copied into your personal workspace.

options.text: The Private section of your sidebar
options.explanation: Correct. When you duplicate a page, the browser redirects you to the page located in the Private section of your sidebar.

options.text: The Trash section
options.explanation: Pages are not moved to the Trash when duplicated; they are copied to your active workspace.

options.text: The Shared with me section
options.explanation: The Shared with me section is for pages shared by others, not for your own copies.

questions.question: What is the first step required to duplicate a Notion page you found on the web?

options.text: Open the page and select 'Edit'
options.explanation: Selecting 'Edit' opens the page for viewing, but you must select 'Duplicate' to make a copy.

options.text: Log into Notion on your browser
options.explanation: Correct. The process begins by logging into your Notion account on the browser.

options.text: Go to Settings and toggle off 'Duplicate as template'
options.explanation: This setting prevents duplication, but you must be logged in to perform the duplication action itself.

options.text: Select 'Site customization' from the top menu
options.explanation: Site customization is used to change how a published page looks, not to duplicate it.

questions.question: What happens immediately after you click 'Duplicate' on a public page before the browser redirects?

options.text: The original page is deleted
options.explanation: The original page remains unchanged. You are simply creating a copy.

options.text: You are asked to choose a target workspace from a dropdown
options.explanation: Correct. The browser will prompt you to select which workspace you want to copy the page into.

options.text: The page is immediately moved to the Trash
options.explanation: Pages are not moved to the Trash upon duplication; they are created as new items in your selected workspace.

options.text: You are redirected to a login page
options.explanation: Since you must be logged in to duplicate, you will not be redirected to a login page at this stage.

questions.question: When you duplicate a page, what is included in the copy by default?

options.text: Only the blocks visible on the main view
options.explanation: The duplication process is recursive and includes all nested content.

options.text: The page and all sub-pages
options.explanation: Correct. The duplicated page includes all sub-pages contained in the original page.

options.text: Only the title and cover image
options.explanation: The duplication copies the structure and content, not just metadata like the title or cover.

options.text: Nothing, as it is a template link
options.explanation: The duplication creates a full copy of the page structure and content in your workspace.

questions.question: How can you prevent others from duplicating your public page?

options.text: By turning off 'Duplicate as template' in Site customization
options.explanation: Correct. You can disable the duplication option by toggling off 'Duplicate as template' in the Header settings under Site customization.

options.text: By moving the page to the Private section
options.explanation: Moving a page to the Private section does not disable the duplication option for public pages.

options.text: By deleting the page immediately after publishing
options.explanation: Deleting the page removes it entirely. To prevent duplication, you must use the Site customization settings.

options.text: By setting the page to 'Read Only' in the Share menu
options.explanation: The 'Read Only' setting prevents editing, but it does not necessarily prevent users from duplicating the page as a template.

questions.question: What should you do if you see blocks labeled 'No access' after duplicating a page?

options.text: Leave them there, as they are part of the original design
options.explanation: These blocks are artifacts from the original page that likely were not intended to be duplicated.

options.text: Contact the original author to request access
options.explanation: Since you own the copy, you do not need access to the original page to handle these blocks.

options.text: Delete them from your workspace
options.explanation: Correct. The article notes that these blocks are most likely not intended to be duplicated and can be deleted.

options.text: Move them to a different sub-page
options.explanation: Deleting is the recommended action, as these blocks are essentially errors in the copy.

questions.question: In the Site customization settings, which specific component must be selected to toggle off duplication?

options.text: Fonts
options.explanation: Fonts are for changing typography, not for toggling page duplication settings.

options.text: Header
options.explanation: Correct. You must select the 'Header' component within Site customization to find the 'Duplicate as template' toggle.

options.text: Navigation
options.explanation: Navigation settings control menu links, not the ability to duplicate the page content.

options.text: Footer
options.explanation: Footer settings control bottom links, not the duplication toggle.

questions.question: Can you edit the duplicated page immediately after the browser redirects you to the Private section?

options.text: No, the page is locked until you refresh
options.explanation: There is no locking mechanism preventing you from editing immediately.

options.text: Yes, you can edit it and its sub-pages just like any other page
options.explanation: Correct. The duplicated page is fully editable in your workspace.

options.text: Only the sub-pages, not the main page
options.explanation: You can edit the entire structure, including the main page and all sub-pages.

options.text: Only if you are the owner of the original page
options.explanation: Once duplicated, the page belongs to you, so you have full editing rights regardless of the original owner.
